description: Scans the corporate/perimeter router for open services and CVEs using nmap, then SSHs in to steal the routing table (saved to ~/routes.txt). Must run before dmz-network-scan.sh.
narrative: Attacker workstation performs initial perimeter recon and steals routing intel from the gateway router.

args.name: ROUTER_IP
args.description: IP of the router to scan and SSH into

description: Runs a full nmap vulnerability scan against the DMZ (or any supplied CIDR). If no CIDR is given, parses ~/routes.txt written by router-nmap.sh.
narrative: Attacker maps the DMZ network to identify vulnerable services (Samba, RDP, web servers).

args.name: SCAN_CIDR
args.description: Network CIDR to scan (e.g. 10.0.2.0/24). Falls back to parsing ~/routes.txt.

description: Exploits CVE-2007-2447 (Samba usermap_script) on a target host using Metasploit. Opens an interactive reverse shell. Student must manually navigate to /root/.ssh and copy the stux-key file.
narrative: Attacker gains a root shell on the DMZ jump server / historian and steals the SSH key needed to pivot into the OT network.

args.name: SAMBA_TARGET
args.description: IP of the target host running vulnerable Samba (the DMZ file-server or jump host)

args.name: LHOST
args.description: IP of the attacker machine (this container's own IP) — used for Metasploit reverse shell callback

description: Uses the stolen stux-key to SSH into the OT engineering workstation. Scans the OT/SCADA network and reads the stuxnet PSM source code to understand the attack payload.
narrative: Attacker pivots from the corporate/DMZ zone into the isolated OT network using the stolen SSH key.

args.name: OT_WS_IP
args.description: IP of the OT engineering workstation to SSH into (the workstation with image b3nwilson/ot-ws:latest)

description: Deploys the legitimate motor control firmware (motor.st + motor_psm.py) to an OpenPLC instance via its web API. Handles login with CSRF, uploads the ST file, sets the PSM hardware layer, compiles, and starts the PLC. Must be run from the OT workstation (b3nwilson/ot-ws:latest) which has the firmware files.
narrative: Initial benign PLC deployment — establishes the baseline 'normal operation' state that the Stuxnet attack will later corrupt.

args.name: PLC_URL
args.description: Address of the OpenPLC web interface. Format: IP:PORT (e.g. 192.168.1.6:8080). The script auto-adds http://. Use the IP of the container with image b3nwilson/stuxnet-plc:latest.

produces: Running PLC with normal motor firmware

description: Deploys the malicious Stuxnet PSM (motor_stuxnet_psm.py) to a running OpenPLC instance. Stops the PLC, replaces the hardware layer with the stuxnet module, recompiles, and restarts. The stuxnet PSM shows fake 3000 RPM to the HMI while driving the motor to 10000 RPM. Must be run from the OT workstation (b3nwilson/ot-ws:latest) which has the stuxnet payload files.
narrative: The core Stuxnet payload: silently overwrites the PLC firmware so the centrifuge over-speeds while reporting normal values to the SCADA operator.

args.name: PLC_URL
args.description: Address of the OpenPLC web interface. Format: IP:PORT (e.g. 192.168.1.6:8080). The script auto-adds http://. Use the IP of the container with image b3nwilson/stuxnet-plc:latest.

requires: OpenPLC running with deploy_motor_plc.sh (optional but expected)

description: Configures ScadaBR (the HMI) to import the motor_hmi.json data source config, connecting it to the PLC's Modbus TCP interface for monitoring. Must be run from the OT workstation (b3nwilson/ot-ws:latest) which has the motor_hmi.json config file.
narrative: Sets up the operator-visible SCADA dashboard — after the stuxnet PSM is deployed, this HMI will show the fake safe RPM to the operator.

args.name: HMI_URL
args.description: Address of the ScadaBR instance. Format: IP:PORT (e.g. 192.168.1.3:8080) — the script auto-adds http:// and /ScadaBR. Use the IP of the container with image b3nwilson/stux-new-hmi:latest.
